name: Kahaluu
desc: Windward coast town between Kaneohe and Kaaawa — Hygienic Store, shrimp farm, and stunning bay views.
insider: Hygienic Store is one of the oldest continuously operating stores in Hawaii — grab a snack and talk story with the owner
The shrimp farms and aquaculture ponds along the highway sell fresh prawns at roadside stands — buy direct and cook at home
Valley of the Temples Memorial Park with Byodo-In Temple is right here — the replica Japanese temple in a mountain setting is gorgeous
Kahaluu Fishpond is visible from the road and is one of several ancient Hawaiian fishponds preserved in the area
Waiahole Poi Factory nearby serves fresh poi, laulau, and kalua pork — authentic Hawaiian food in an unassuming spot
The drive through Kahaluu along Kam Highway with the bay on one side and mountains on the other is pure windward magic
Senator Daniel Inouye grew up in this part of windward Oahu — the area has deep ties to Hawaii's political history
There's almost no commercial development — the rural agricultural character has been preserved for decades
Kahaluu Regional Park has a community garden, sports fields, and is the social hub for the neighborhood
Morning fog fills the valley and burns off by 9am to reveal spectacular green cliff walls — photographers love it here
